title: Vault path: natural 20 never deals critical damage (players AND monsters)
Vault path: player damage formula is fixed 1d6 + full to-hit bonus (incl. proficiency), ignoring the actual weapon
Vault path: death saving throws are unreachable — PC at 0 HP gets no dying/unconscious state
Massive-damage instant death computed but never persisted — dead PC remains merely unconscious
PC dropping to 0 HP never receives the 'unconscious' condition; a 0-HP PC can still act
Opportunity attacks cannot be legally resolved in the baked path
Vault path: initiative is a flat 1d20+0 for both PCs and monsters (DEX modifier dropped)
Raw apply_damage mutations bypass all 0-HP rules; attack.ts drops isCrit from the mutation
Loading weapon property is bypassed for Extra Attack — exactly the case it exists to restrict
Temporary HP ignored by the vault hp_change reducer and the monster-turn loop
Vault combat ignores conditions, advantage/disadvantage, cover, and resistances entirely
Sneak Attack implemented in the engine but unreachable from the tool surface
No deterministic combat termination in the engine path; endTurn cycles dead encounters forever
Ranged-attack range bands and ranged-in-melee disadvantage not enforced despite position tracking
Resistance + vulnerability to the same damage type: vulnerability silently dropped
set_temp_hp mutation ignores actorId — always writes the PC session row
Help action grants advantage on ANY next attack, without the within-5-ft target constraint
Caster spell slots never progress past level 1 — levelUp() never emits newSlots and nothing re-derives slotsMax
Hit-dice pool frozen at 1 forever — hit_dice_max never updated on level-up
Multiclass level grants no HP, no hit die, and stale proficiency bonus — and blocks the level_up tool afterwards
Warlock Pact Magic slots are not restored on short rest
Short-rest hit-die healing missing the per-die minimum of 1 — negative CON mod can produce negative healing and silently waste dice
Level-up HP roll not clamped to minimum 1 per level — characters can lose max HP on level-up
apply_condition tool can never target the player character (baked path) — conditions on the PC silently impossible
Bardic Inspiration recharges on short rest at all bard levels (should be level 5+ only)
Arcane Recovery recharges on every short rest instead of once per day
Barbarian gets unlimited rages at level 17 instead of level 20
Condition speed/HP-max effects are computed but enforced nowhere (grappled/restrained can walk; exhaustion 2/4/5 inert; paralyzed/stunned/petrified flag table missing speed 0)
Contested and group checks ignore condition/exhaustion effects (no runtime plumbed through)
recomputeAC silently ignores its armorSpecs parameter — any armor outside the 13-slug hardcoded catalog is treated as unarmored
level_up tool enforces no XP threshold — the LLM can level the PC at any time, by multiple levels
Single-class half-caster slot progression rounds down (rules.md §13.2 text) which under-allocates odd-level paladins/rangers vs the PHB class tables
Forced-march/dehydration saves roll a raw d20 instead of going through savingThrow(), bypassing condition effects
Unconscious does not auto-apply prone, so ranged attacks against an unconscious target get pure advantage instead of the RAW net-normal roll
Spell slot progression never happens: all casters keep level-1 slots forever
Every attack-roll spell fails through the cast_spell tool: target AC is stripped
Save spells apply FULL damage before the save; heal-refund protocol cannot undo deaths, death-save failures, or concentration breaks
Concentration never tracked for unbound spells; spellMeta.concentration fetched but ignored, while the prompt forbids manual tracking
Warlock Pact Magic slots never recover on short rest and have no level progression
Spell save DC and spell attack bonus frozen at character creation (stale at level 5+)
Scorching Ray binding: one ray instead of three, no upcast scaling
spell_action meta-tool input contract mismatches the cast_spell handler (spell vs spellSlug, target vs targets)
Prepared-caster rule not enforced: spellsPrepared exists but is never checked
Bonus-action spell rule (8.5) wrong in both directions
Faerie Fire applies the defensive 'shielded' buff to enemies that FAIL the save
Buff conditions (blessed/baned/shielded/mage-armored) have no mechanical effect despite code comments claiming roll-site/AC handling
Sleep binding invents a WIS save; RAW sleep has no save (5d8 HP pool)
Disintegrate marked halfOnSuccess; RAW save negates entirely
Several damage bindings significantly under RAW (missing second damage component or spell mod)
33 SPELL_BINDINGS keys unreachable: PHB-name slugs mismatch SRD CSV slugs, and several SRD spells missing from spells.csv
Ritual casting: no class-feature gate and free upcast scaling on ritual casts
Becoming incapacitated via a condition (no damage) does not break concentration
Multi-beam cantrip scaling modeled as one big attack roll instead of separate beams
Inconsistent duration units in bindings (rounds vs seconds) and one 10x duration error
Latent crit bug: flat damage modifier would be doubled on critical hits
combat.md materialized view omits the monster `cr` field
notes: Code facts verified accurate: EncounterState.monsters[].cr exists (projector.ts:675, populated at 802/815) and serializeCombatView (projector.ts:1295-1320) never emits it. But this is intentional, documented design, not a state-consistency bug. (1) The combat.md view shape is LOCKED from phase 06 WITHOUT cr (.planning/phases/06-vault-combat-state-foundation-d1/06-CONTEXT.md:67-69 lists monsters fields as id/name/hpCurrent/hpMax/ac?/initiativeBonus?/isAlive/conditions). (2) D-08 (.planning/phases/09-v2-monster-turns/09-01-PLAN.md Task 2) added cr strictly additively to schema+reducer ONLY, with the explicit purpose "so the resolver can read it at attack time"; the planned chain (schema → reducer → CR table → resolver → loop → route → narration) never includes combat.md serialization, and the in-code comment at projector.ts:673-674 states the same consumer. (3) The LLM is the PRODUCER of cr, not a consumer — prompt-builder.ts:238-239 instructs it to include cr in monster_spawn "the server uses it to set the monster's attack strength"; no prompt logic reads cr back from combat.md, and for SRD monsters the LLM can read cr from handbook/monsters/<slug>.md frontmatter anyway. (4) REQ-004 (.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md:12) only requires views to be materialized from events.md, not to serialize every state field; the finding's "REQ-004 intent" fidelity claim overreaches. (5) Resolver path confirmed unaffected: route.ts:561 replays events.md; monster-turns.ts:395 reads activeMonster.cr from replayed state. Player impact on the current default path (vault backend): zero — all combat math flows through events.md replay. Implementing the recommendation would deviate from the LOCKED view contract for no functional gain. At most a cosmetic/documentation enhancement, not a bug.

title: Vault per-turn directives order apply_event tool calls on turns where the route removes the tool surface
Vault system-prompt Combat-lifecycle block contradicts the server-authoritative combat design (stale Phase-07 instructions)
English vault campaigns cannot resolve combat: prompt teaches EN roll phrasings but the server resolver and all injected directives are Italian-only
Weak-tool (gemma) models receive a full tool-advertising system prompt while the route strips all tools on every turn
Compact DM handbook teaches a wrong death-save rule: 'Crit on death save = +2 successes'
Legacy path with a non-baked local model overflows OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=16384 (~25K static tokens; tool contract alone ~14.8K)
save_half refund instruction makes targets take round-UP half damage (off-by-one vs round-down convention)
Exploration mode block instructs a tool call the schema rejects: set_travel_pace with pace=null
Baked SLIM tool contract and OOC block reference tools the active surface does not expose (make_attack et al. on meta-tools; nonexistent set_combat)
Opening-turn contradictions on the vault path: system prompt mandates tool exploration and proactive roll-asking while the begin directive and loop forbid both
Concentration example in tool contract names fireball, which is not a concentration spell
Wrong rulebook citation: currency conversion attributed to 'PHB §5.2' (which is Long Rest in rules.md; currency is §9.2)
Starvation survival days: prompt says '3 + CON mod' while rules.md says 'CON mod days, minimum 1' (ground-truth file likely dropped the '3 +')
Three mutually inconsistent XP-award scales coexist across prompt blocks
Crafting time formula in tool contract deviates ~20x from PHB RAW (days = ceil(price × 2))
